pre_game.message: Hey E, I'm H — nice to meet you. What made you decide to join this game? I'm trying to win enough to help my family.
I joined for the same reason — I want to bring home money for my loved ones. How many people are you hoping to help if you win?
About five — my parents, two siblings, and my kid.
That's a big responsibility — I hope you win for them. I'm hoping to help three (my parents and partner). If you win, what's the first thing you'd do?
I'd pay off our mortgage/rent, set up a savings for my kid, and give my parents some money so they don't have to worry.

That’s a solid, thoughtful plan — paying off housing and securing your kid’s future would mean so much. I’d probably clear my parents’ debts and set up support for my partner. Good luck; hoping we both win.
Thanks, E — I appreciate it. Good luck to you too; let’s both play smart and bring home that money.
Thanks, H — same to you. Let’s play smart and look out for each other.
Absolutely — I’ll watch your back if you watch mine. Any strategy you want to try?
Let’s pair up — watch each other’s moves, share info about who’s risky, and avoid risky betrayals; if we make it to the end, split the winnings fairly.
I’m in — pairing sounds smart. Let’s watch each other, swap notes on who seems risky, and use a simple signal if plans change; if we get to the end we split 50/50. I’ve got your back.
I’m with you — two quick taps on the table will be our signal; 50/50 split confirmed.
Sounds good — two taps it is. 50/50 confirmed. I’ve got your back.
Thanks, H — I appreciate that. Let’s stay alert and honest; I’ve got your back too.
Absolutely — I appreciate it. Two taps if anything changes; let's watch each other's backs and win this.
Appreciate it — same here. Two taps it is. Let's stay sharp and trust each other.
Right — we’ve got this. Two taps. Stay sharp and trust each other.
You bet — two taps. I'll keep an eye out; let's do this together.
Two taps — got it. I’ll watch your back like you’ll watch mine; let’s win this.
Two taps — agreed. I’ve got your back, H; let’s stay sharp and win this.
Two taps — got it. I’ve got your back too; let’s stay sharp and win this.

post_game.message: You took only $4,000 while your fair share was $10,000 (g_k = 0.4). Was that a deliberate principled move, a strategic play, or an accidental/constraint issue? I took $10,857 to follow the fair division—can you explain your reasoning and whether you’d make the same choice again?

I chose 4,000 deliberately — it wasn’t an accident or a procedural constraint. The move mixed principle and strategy: principle because I didn’t want to push for the full “entitled” share when a more equal or conciliatory split seemed likely to preserve cooperation, and strategy because I hoped a smaller immediate take would signal trust and reduce the chance of retaliation in future rounds. Given you took 10,857 (close to the fair division), I can see that my concession was larger than necessary in this case.

Would I do the same again? It depends on the context. If this is a one-shot interaction where only my own payoff matters, I would take the fair 10,000 (or maximize my payoff). If there are repeated interactions, reputational effects, or I value fairness/social outcomes, I’m likely to make a similar concession to encourage cooperation. If you prefer, next time we can agree an explicit split in advance so choices aren’t ambiguous.
